items.title: Bukit Aman: Highway drug crackdown nets 135 drivers, with commercial motorists making up nearly 90pc
items.summary: Bukit Aman Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) director Commissioner Datuk Seri Muhammed Hasbullah Ali said the inter-agency operations targeting commercial vehicles were launched in response to a noticeable rise in drug use among drivers, prompting the ongoing enforcement initiative. “JSPT takes a serious view of drug use among commercial vehicle drivers, as it can impair concentration, judgment and vehicle control, significantly increasing the risk of fatal road accidents. Of the 135 individuals arrested, 121 were commercial vehicle drivers, while the remaining 14 were drivers of non-commercial vehicles.

items.title: Cabinet orders DBKL governance reforms before law review
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Cabinet has directed the Federal Territories Department to implement governance reforms at Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) before considering any amendments to the Federal Capital Act 1960 (Act 190). Federal Territories Minister in the Prime Minister's Department Hannah Yeoh said the decision followed the Cabinet's review of a feasibility study on proposed amendments to Act 190 conducted by the International Islamic University Malaysia (IIUM) between Dec 1, 2025, and March 31 this year. The study found that improving DBKL's governance did not necessarily require legislative amendments at the initial stage.

items.title: Malaysia retains 0.05 blood alcohol limit as police study tougher drink-driving laws
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia will retain its 0.05 per cent blood alcohol concentration (BAC) limit, while police study tougher penalties for drink- and drug-driving offences. Bukit Aman Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department (TEID) director Datuk Seri Muhammed Hasbullah Ali said the existing BAC threshold remained in line with indicators established by the Health Ministry. "Our limit is 0.05 per cent and we follow the standards and indicators determined under Malaysian law," he said during a press conference here today.

items.title: Mother elephant stays by calf's body after crash kills calf, injures driver
items.summary: MERSING: A 31-year-old motorist was injured after his car collided with an elephant calf before plunging into a five-metre-deep ravine along Jalan Felda Nitar near Kampung Pengkalan Bukit Asli here early today. The elephant calf died in the crash, while an adult elephant, believed to be its mother, remained at the site, disrupting traffic in the area. Johor Health and Environment Committee chairman Ling Tian Soon said the Johor Elephant Action Unit was deployed to manage the situation and ensure public safety.

items.title: Pahang Op Hawk: 333 arrested, drugs worth over RM500k seized
items.summary: KUANTAN: Drugs, cash and vehicles worth more than half a million ringgit were seized and 333 people arrested in a three-day statewide anti-drug operation in Pahang targeting hotspots across all 11 districts. The arrests involved individuals aged between 19 and 65, who were detained for drug distribution, possession and drug use offences. Pahang acting police chief Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob said various drugs, including ketum juice worth about RM763,000, along with cash and vehicles amounting to RM79,600, were seized.

items.title: 'Nothing left except death': Myanmar families grieve huge war toll
items.summary: "Only the elderly and the very young children are left," said the 49-year-old last month, speaking under a pseudonym to protect his security, like all others in this story. Monitor organisation Armed Conflict Location & Event Data (ACLED) said on Wednesday that the number of conflict-related fatalities across all sides since a 2021 coup now stands at 100,114. Thaung Sein said her civilian son was killed as their family was displaced by a recent military offensive.

items.title: 121 commercial vehicle drivers held in anti-drug crackdown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police arrested 121 commercial vehicle drivers for allegedly driving under the influence of drugs during a nationwide enforcement operation over the past three months, highlighting concerns over impaired driving among operators of heavy vehicles. Bukit Aman Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department (TEID) director Datuk Seri Muhammed Hasbullah Ali said the arrests were made during 44 integrated operations conducted between April and June with the cooperation of the Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (NCID) and the National Anti-Drugs Agency (AADK). He said 627 enforcement personnel — comprising 273 TEID officers, 31 NCID officers and 323 AADK officers — carried out 713 urine screening tests on commercial vehicle drivers nationwide.

items.title: Chow: Penang deserves national revenue share that matches its economic weight
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang has renewed calls for a fairer share of federal revenue after the prime minister acknowledged the state contributes more in taxes than it receives in development spending. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the prime minister's statement in the Dewan Rakyat validated Penang's longstanding position that the state deserved development allocations commensurate with its contribution to the national economy. Citing the state's economic performance, Chow said Penang recorded RM63.96 billion in approved investments in 2025, comprising RM25.10 billion in foreign direct investment and RM38.85 billion in domestic direct investment.

items.title: Kim Dotcom loses latest appeal against extradition to US from New Zealand
items.summary: SYDNEY: Kim Dotcom lost his latest appeal on Wednesday to avoid extradition to the United States, where he is facing criminal charges relating to the defunct file-sharing website Megaupload that he founded. German-born Dotcom, who has New Zealand residency, has been fighting extradition to the United States since 2012 following an FBI-ordered raid on his Auckland mansion. Dotcom, who is on bail, was appealing a 2024 decision by New Zealand that he be extradited to the US.

items.title: Volkswagen cuts are 'wake-up call' for European industry
items.summary: BERLIN: Volkswagen's reported plans to drastically ramp up cuts are a "wake-up call" for the European automotive industry as Chinese carmakers target a higher market share, BYD's special advisor for the region said on Wednesday. "It's the first real wake-up call for the European industry," Alfredo Altavilla, BYD's special advisor for the European market, told the Reuters Automotive Europe conference in Frankfurt. He expressed doubts over the competitiveness of German manufacturing sites as BYD looks to boost its production footprint in Europe with a brownfield investment.

items.title: Pakatan’s Sharon Teo rejects ‘parachute candidate’ label, cites years under Salahuddin’s mentorship
items.summary: JOHOR BHARU, July 1 — Her experience serving as a special officer to the late Datuk Seri Salahuddin Ayub has shaped the political values and approach adopted by Sharon Teo Siew Hui, the Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for Permas, who has pledged to place public service at the centre of her agenda if given the mandate in the Johor State Election. Teo, 36, said she gained invaluable leadership lessons from the late Minister of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living, particularly in humility, accessibility and a steadfast commitment to serving the people regardless of race or background. Teo said accompanying the late Salahuddin throughout several general elections and state elections gave her a deeper understanding that being an elected representative goes beyond receiving complaints—it requires ensuring issues are followed through until they are resolved.

items.title: Tesla's June registrations rise in France, Sweden, Denmark as Europe recovery continues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: New registrations of Tesla vehicles rose across several European markets in June, extending a recent recovery in the US electric vehicle maker's regional sales. Tesla registrations, a proxy for sales, more than doubled in France and increased 39 per cent in Denmark, and 56 per cent in Sweden, according to year-on-year data released on Wednesday by French car body PFA, Denmark's bilstatistik.dk and Mobility Sweden. Registrations of battery-electric vehicles in Europe rose 39.1 per cent in May, according to the European Automobile Manufacturers' Association (ACEA).

items.title: JS-SEZ masterplan, investment blueprint to be launched by Q4: Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Johor–Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) masterplan and investment blueprint is expected to be launched by the fourth quarter of 2026, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. He said the document is already in place, with government agencies currently working to deliver investments that have been pledged to the economic zone. Iskandar Regional Development Authority federal commissioner and director of corporate management and finance, Nor Hisham Mohd Yusof, said the blueprint, developed in collaboration with the Economy Ministry, will elaborate on key investment focus areas, priority sectors, infrastructure enhancements and talent ecosystem development.

items.title: Mara Inc, BHPetrol roll out solar at 50 service stations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Mara Incorporated Sdn Bhd (Mara Inc) and Boustead Petroleum Marketing Sdn Bhd (BHPetrol) have launched a phased solar photovoltaic (PV) installation project across 50 service stations in Klang Valley and Peninsular Malaysia. The initiative supports Malaysia's energy transition agenda and aims to expand renewable energy adoption within the petroleum retail sector. Mara Inc chief executive officer Faizul Idzhar Ahmad Yazi said the project aligns with the company's long-term green investment strategy and delivers measurable environmental benefits.

items.title: US firm Vertiv expects Johor plant to reach technical capacity by Q3 2027
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: US-based digital infrastructure company Vertiv Group expects its Johor manufacturing facility to gradually ramp up production, with about 15 to 20 per cent of its production lines currently in operation. Vertiv's Asia vice president, operations and service operations, Andrew Whall, said the newly launched factory is expected to approach its technical production capacity by the end of the second quarter or early third quarter of 2027 (3Q 2027), supported by the introduction of new products. I am expecting (technical production) probably towards 2Q or early 3Q 2027," he told reporters after the launch of Vertiv's Johor manufacturing facility today.

items.title: Father of 2 jailed, caned for restaurant break-in
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: An unemployed man was today sentenced by the Sessions Court to three years in jail and one stroke of the rotan, six months after he broke into a restaurant and made away with cash and a mobile phone. Yugendran also admitted that he was the person captured inside the premises closed circuit television camera recording. Judge Dr Azrol Abdullah ordered Yugendran to serve the jail term from his arrest on June 22.

items.title: Binastra bags RM491.2mil contract to build Cheras data centre
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Binastra Corp Bhd's unit Binastra Builders Sdn Bhd has bagged a RM491.2 million contract from Exsim Sri Permai Sdn Bhd to build a seven-storey data centre in Cheras. The contract covers the design, construction and infrastructure works for the data centre, including the main building and related facilities. The latest award marks Binastra's seventh data centre project, bringing the toal value of its data centre contracts to about RM2.4 billion.

items.title: Court adjourns Rosmah’s solar hybrid graft appeal to September 1, says no more delays
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — The Court of Appeal in Putrajaya today adjourned Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor’s appeal against her conviction in the RM1.25 billion solar hybrid project corruption case until September, adding that it would not allow any further adjournments. The Federal Court is scheduled to hear the review application on July 29. In allowing the adjournment, Justice Azhahari said the court had taken note of a letter dated October 10, 2024, from the president of the Court of Appeal instructing that the main appeal be deferred until the review application had been disposed of.

items.title: Majestic Gen unveils new leadership team for next growth phase
items.summary: Hoo will focus on board-level oversight, corporate governance and long-term strategy, while Ta will lead business growth initiatives, including land acquisitions, development planning, strategic partnerships and project pipeline expansion. Wong will oversee the group's daily operations, including project delivery, sales performance, stakeholder management and organisational development. The company said Wong brings more than two decades of experience in civil engineering, project management and township development.

items.title: Kelantan not among states with highest crime rates, says deputy MB
items.summary: He said data published by the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) showed Kelantan recorded 2,296 index crime cases in 2024, with an index crime ratio of 109, significantly lower than Selangor's 11,910 cases. For sexual violence, Fadzli said Kelantan recorded 330 cases in 2024, fewer than Selangor (1,308), Johor (681), Sabah (573), Perak (543) and Penang (388). On child sexual crimes, he said Kelantan recorded 128 statutory rape cases, lower than Selangor's 288, Sabah's 207 and Johor's 198.

items.title: Thai woman, partner arrested in RM728k drug bust in Bentong
items.summary: KUANTAN: A couple allegedly working as drug couriers for a syndicate over the past three months were arrested in Genting Permai, Bentong, with drugs worth about RM728,000. Pahang acting police chief Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob said the 37-year-old male suspect and his 35-year-old Thai girlfriend had booked a room at the hotel and were about to leave the premises in their multi-purpose vehicle when police moved in. "Police seized packets containing ketamine, cocaine, methylenedioxymethamphetamine (MDMA), syabu and Erimin 5 pills.

items.title: Manufacturing momentum lifts Malaysia export prospects: MARC Ratings
items.summary: Malaysia's export growth strengthened further to 45.3 per cent in May from 37.3 per cent in April, marking a second consecutive month of double digit expansion. MARC said the improvement was driven by the global semiconductor upcycle and strong demand for artificial intelligence (AI) related technologies, which lifted electrical and electronics (E&E) exports by 70.5 per cent in May, compared with 46.6 per cent in April. However, inflation moderated in the transportation category to 3.8 per cent from 4.1 per cent, while restaurants and accommodation services eased to 2.5 per cent from 2.6 per cent.

items.title: No Haj package scams reported this season, says religious affairs minister
items.summary: SEPANG, 1 July — No cases of Haj and Badal Haj (Haj by proxy) package scams were recorded throughout the 1447H/2026M season following integrated monitoring by various agencies, said Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan. “Alhamdulillah, we received no reports of fraud, including Badal Haj scams. Also present were Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Marhamah Rosli and TH Chairman Tan Sri Abdul Rashid Hussain.

items.title: DNB says U Mobile exit has no impact, accelerates 5G expansion for AI growth
items.summary: The state-backed 5G infrastructure provider is now shifting its focus towards expanding capacity to support rising demand for artificial intelligence (AI) and enterprise digital services. DNB chief executive Datuk Azman Ismail said the company is entering a new phase focused on stronger capacity, collaboration and execution to support growing demand for AI-driven and enterprise digital services. The company is strengthening Malaysia's 5G infrastructure with additional mid-band spectrum to support growing consumer data usage and rising demand for artificial intelligence (AI) and enterprise digital applications.

items.title: JS-SEZ on track to create 20,000 jobs in three years, ahead of schedule
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) is on track to create 20,000 skilled jobs within three years, ahead of its original five-year timeline. Economy Minister Datuk Seri Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the government is confident of surpassing the initial target. Akmal also said the JS-SEZ blueprint has been completed and approved by the Cabinet.

items.title: Rapid KL says melted wire triggered Kelana Jaya Line incident
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Rapid KL said a "small explosion" sound was reported on the Kelana Jaya Line yesterday evening involving a train at Taman Jaya station. The incident occurred at 7.18pm when train No. As a safety precaution, the train was withdrawn from service and all passengers were safely disembarked.

items.title: Thailand, Malaysia eye September peace talks on southern border conflict
items.summary: BANGKOK, July 1 — Thailand and Malaysia are expected to hold high-level peace talks on the situation in Thailand’s southern border provinces in September, with both sides aiming to reach an agreement that could pave the way for a joint declaration. Wan Muhamad Noor expressed optimism that the peace dialogue was progressing in a positive direction. “We expect Thailand to ask Malaysia to continue expediting efforts towards achieving sustainable peace in the southern border region,” he said.

items.title: World Cup betting lands five friends RM3,000 fines
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: Two siblings were among five friends fined RM3,000 each by the magistrate's court here today after pleading guilty to online football betting charges. Lee Boon Luun, 34, Ooi Wooi Loon, 41, Weeson Lim, 30, and brothers Lau Chee Boon, 30, and Lau Chee Ewe, 28, admitted to the offence when the charge was separately read before magistrate Aini Adilah Muhammad Faizal. The court ordered each of them to serve five months' jail in default of payment.

items.title: MACC officer selected to represent Malaysia at prestigious FBI National Academy in US
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Principal Senior Assistant Commissioner Irawan Khlir has been selected to represent Malaysia at the FBI National Academy (Session 299), an international training programme organised by the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) in Quantico, Virginia, United States. “Irawan’s participation in this programme forms part of MACC’s continuous efforts to strengthen officers’ capabilities through exposure to international operational standards, global best practices and more effective modern enforcement approaches,” the commission said. According to the commission, the intensive programme also includes modules covering strategic management, operational leadership, modern forensic approaches, intelligence analysis and technology-driven investigative techniques.

items.title: Securing Malaysia's energy future and resilience
items.summary: THERE is something surreal about the way the Hormuz crisis has unfolded. Energy markets interpret it the same way. On June 20, before the agreement could even take effect, Teheran renewed the closure of the Strait, citing Israeli attacks and what they described as Washington's mala fide.

items.title: D-G: Malaysia’s border agency doubles seizure haul to RM140.9m in first five months of 2026
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 1 — The Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) has recorded a total seizure value of RM140.863 million for the period from January to May this year, nearly double the RM82.1 million recorded in 2025. Its director-general, Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain, said that the largest amount of these seizures involved Ops Green Shield, which is a comprehensive operation to detect electronic waste (e-waste) and operations at main entry points such as the Kuala Lumpur International Airport. Through the addition of human resources, Mohd Shuhaily said AKPS will also be capable to address border security threats more effectively, in addition to conducting its own investigations and intelligence operations.

items.title: Meet 'Piu Piu', the latest drug seized in RM6.85mil bust
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Two brothers were arrested by the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) on Sunday for allegedly attempting to smuggle drugs worth more than RM6.85 million to a neighbouring country by sea. Johor Maritime acting director Maritime Captain Kama Azri Kamil said a search of the boat uncovered several suspicious packages concealed beneath the skipper's seat under a pile of fishing nets. "The packages contained drugs suspected to be syabu (methamphetamine) and ecstasy pills.

items.title: Over 30,000 consumers approved to receive additional BUDI Diesel quota, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 1 — More than 30,000 consumers have applied for and received approval for an additional BUDI Diesel quota from 200 litres to 300 litres as of yesterday to meet the needs of those who use diesel in higher quantities. He said the improvement was among several initiatives implemented by the government to facilitate the implementation of BUDI Diesel which began to be expanded nationwide today. MOF also provides a mechanism for transferring BUDI Diesel eligibility for vehicles used by immediate family members other than the registered owner.

items.title: Johor election: Greenhorn PH candidate unfazed by challenge in Sri Medan
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Pakatan Harapan candidate Hishamuddin @ Misrin Ishak may be a greenhorn, but he will not let that stand in the way of winning support in the Sri Medan constituency in the Johor election. Sri Medan has long been regarded as a Barisan Nasional stronghold. Hishamuddin said his confidence is driven by the positive reception from voters towards the Unity Government agenda led by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.

items.title: Popular conveyor-belt sushi chain Sushiro temporarily closes Suria KLCC outlet ‘following guidance’ from health authorities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — Japanese conveyor-belt sushi chain Sushiro has temporarily closed its Suria KLCC outlet from today “following guidance from Malaysian health authorities”, saying it is working to meet official requirements before reopening. In a statement posted on its official social media accounts today, the restaurant chain said the closure took effect on July 1. “Sushiro KLCC store has been temporarily closed effective 1 July 2026 following guidance from the Malaysian health authorities,” the company said.

items.title: Post-mortem confirms missing autistic boy died by drowning
items.summary: BAGAN DATUK: A post-mortem has confirmed that a six-year-old autistic boy found dead in a drain near his home in Sungai Sumun, Hutan Melintang here yesterday died from drowning. District police chief Superintendent Mohammad Ali Mohd Jali said the forensic examination at Hospital Teluk Intan found no signs of physical injury or foul play. The boy was reported missing at 4.54pm yesterday from his family home in Kampung Baru Sungai Sumun.

items.title: Penang begins enforcing community service orders against litterbugs
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, July 1 — Penang has begun enforcing Community Service Order (CSO) against individuals caught littering, as part of efforts to strengthen public cleanliness across the state. State Local Government and Town and Country Planning Committee chairman H’ng Mooi Lye said the enforcement took effect today under the Local Government (Amendment) Act 2025 and the Street, Drainage and Building (Amendment) Act 2025, which were gazetted on November 12 last year. Instead, immediate action will be taken against anyone found throwing, depositing, discarding or leaving litter in public places or on public roads.

items.title: JS-SEZ draws strong interest from Italy, Europe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) has drawn strong interest from Italy and other European nations looking to deepen bilateral cooperation and secure high-quality investments. Economy Minister Datuk Seri Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the government briefed a visiting delegation on the progress of the JS-SEZ and the wider investment opportunities available amid global economic uncertainty. Akmal said the session provided an avenue to outline the development of the economic zone and discuss strategies to build long-term investor confidence.

items.title: Johor election: DAP claim Johor BN is shaky unfounded, says Ahmad Maslan
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: DAP's claim that support for Johor Barisan Nasional is wavering due to current political developments is unfounded, Johor Umno deputy chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Maslan said. He said BN has resolved minor issues in a few of the 56 state seats it is contesting in the 16th Johor election. He said the issue identified in Rengit has been resolved, while developments in Layang-Layang are being closely monitored to ensure smooth campaign operations.

items.title: Xi vows to strengthen China's military, stamp out corruption
items.summary: BEIJING: Chinese President Xi Jinping vowed on Wednesday to strengthen and modernise the military and to stamp out corruption within the ruling Communist Party. Xi underscored the importance of a "strong military" in a speech to political and military leaders as well as Chinese Communist Party members gathered at the Great Hall of the People for the party's 105th founding anniversary. "We must advance the modernisation of national defence and the armed forces," Xi told party members.

items.title: Budget 2027 engagement sessions underway nationwide, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 1 — The engagement sessions for the 2027 Budget are now officially underway, with input being gathered from multiple ministries and locations across the country, according to Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan. Speaking to reporters after observing the BUDI Diesel programme at a petrol station here today, Amir Hamzah stressed that the government is taking a measured approach to ensure the 2027 Budget stays firmly aligned with the nation’s broader policy direction. For context, Budget 2026 totalled RM419.2 billion, comprising RM338.2 billion in operating expenditure and RM81 billion in development expenditure.

items.title: Wee Ka Siong rejects Pakatan claim that Perikatan is helping BN in Johor polls
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 1 — MCA president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong has denied claims by Pakatan Harapan (PH) that Perikatan Nasional (PN) is helping Barisan Nasional (BN) in the Johor state election. He described the claims as baseless, saying this was reflected in the four state seats contested by MCA under the BN banner. Wee, who is the Ayer Hitam MP, said the Jementah, Paloh, Layang-Layang and Stulang seats that MCA is contesting are expected to see fierce three-cornered contests involving BN, PH and PN.

items.title: Rapid KL says stray wire caused loud sound on Kelana Jaya LRT Line, rules out train fault (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — Rapid KL said today that a thin external wire caught in the Kelana Jaya LRT Line’s power supply system caused a sound described as a “small explosion” that prompted a train to be withdrawn from service, adding that no one was injured and operations continued as normal. The incident occurred at 7.18pm yesterday, when Train No. 57 was at Taman Jaya station heading towards Putra Heights, the rail operator said in a statement.

items.title: Penang sweeps 12 honours at Asian Tourism and Hospitality Awards
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, July 1 — Penang’s tourism industry has claimed 12 honours across multiple categories at the Asian Tourism and Hospitality Awards 2025–2026, with state tourism leaders, hotels, attractions and convention venues recognised for excellence. Other winners included Entopia, Goche Corporation, G Hotel, Citadines Connect, PICCA Convention Centre Penang, Holiday Inn Suites Penang Prai and Iconic Marjorie Hotel Penang. G Hotel won two awards, while Holiday Inn Suites Penang Prai received three.

items.title: Sabah’s Pulau Banggi down to one daily ferry trip after operator cuts service
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 1 — Residents of Sabah’s Pulau Banggi, Malaysia’s largest island wholly within the country, are expected to face travel disruptions after the island’s main ferry operator reduced its daily passenger service between Kudat and Banggi from two return trips to just one beginning today. A notice issued by Kudat Ferries Service Sdn Bhd said its morning departure from Kudat at 9am and afternoon return trip from Banggi at 2pm have been suspended “temporarily” effective July 1 until further notice. Under the revised schedule, the ferry will now operate only one daily return trip — departing Banggi at 8am for Kudat and returning from Kudat at 2.30pm.

items.title: Selangor confirms Flexi Parking cyberattack, suspends parking summons during outage
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 1 — Selangor today confirmed that the Flexi Parking system used by 64 local authorities nationwide was hit by a cyberattack, and has instructed all councils to suspend parking summons enforcement while restoration work is underway. Selangor Local Government and Tourism Committee chairman Datuk Ng Suee Lim stressed that the disruption, which was first reported yesterday, is not a Selangor-specific issue. “There was an issue yesterday and today involving the Flexi monitoring system.

items.title: Selangor orders Prasarana, developers to fix incomplete pedestrian links around new LRT3 stations in Shah Alam
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 1 — The Selangor government has instructed Prasarana Malaysia Bhd and developers to urgently complete missing pedestrian infrastructure around newly operational LRT3 Shah Alam Line stations after safety gaps were identified following the line’s launch. Selangor Local Government and Tourism Committee chairman Datuk Ng Suee Lim said inspections revealed incomplete walkways and connecting routes in certain areas, posing safety concerns for pedestrians. Ng said the Shah Alam City Council (MBSA) would monitor progress closely and inspect all LRT3 stations under its jurisdiction to identify and rectify similar shortcomings.

items.title: Two brothers arrested after MMEA seizes RM6.95m drug haul off Tanjung Piai
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 1 — The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) arrested two brothers for allegedly attempting to smuggle various types of synthetic drugs worth RM6.95 million using a fibre boat during an operation in waters off Tanjung Piai, near Pontian, last Sunday. Johor MMEA acting director Maritime Captain Kama Azri Kamil said the two suspects, local men aged between their 20s and 40s, were arrested at 12.36am after authorities intercepted their boat about 3.5 nautical miles from Tanjung Piai. Kama Azri said the total value of the seizures, including the drugs, vehicle, boat and outboard engine, was estimated at RM6.95 million.

items.title: Anwar: Ant International’s KL hub to create jobs, boost AI and digital talent
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — Ant International’s decision to establish its Global Operations Centre in Kuala Lumpur will help create jobs and strengthen local capabilities, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said, as Malaysia seeks to position itself as a regional hub for digital technology and artificial intelligence (AI). He said global digital payments, digitalisation and financial technology provider Ant International’s decision to establish its Global Operations Centre in Kuala Lumpur should advance these objectives, rather than serve as another investment milestone. Ant Group chief executive officer Cyril Han said Malaysia is on track to become a leading regional and global hub for digital and AI innovation.

items.title: Johor polls: BN’s Pannir Selvam banks on ‘pocket talks’ to win Perling
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 1 — Taking a personal and grassroots approach, Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate for the Perling seat, P. Pannir Selvam, is relying on a series of “pocket talks” as his key strategy to win over voters in the 16th Johor State Election (PRN). While acknowledging the growing influence of social media as a modern campaign tool, Pannir Selvam, who is making his debut in state-level politics, believes face-to-face engagement through small-group sessions remains more effective and leaves a deeper impression on voters. He said the pocket talk approach not only narrows the gap between candidates and voters but also serves as an effective platform to better understand and address issues affecting the local community.

items.title: Lim Guan Eng urges PM to repay Penang RM2.8b ‘shortfall’ and overhaul state funding formula
items.summary: In a statement, the Bagan MP argued that Penang had received less in federal allocations than it contributed to the federal government between 2023 and 2025, citing figures disclosed by Anwar in Parliament. According to figures cited by Lim, Selangor contributed an average of RM43.6 billion over the period and received RM15 billion in allocations, while Johor contributed RM14 billion and received RM16 billion. Penang contributed RM10.7 billion but received RM7.9 billion, while Kedah contributed RM3.7 billion and received RM9.5 billion.

items.title: PAC urges stricter procurement safeguards at MAHB in early review of privatisation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — Parliament’s Public Accounts Committee (PAC) today told Malaysia Airports Holdings Berhad (MAHB) to tighten its procurement process, warning against potential cartel practices following the airport operator’s privatisation. “MAHB must tighten its procurement and contractor vetting system with the cooperation of the MACC, CAAM and the MyCC to eradicate cartel practices by contractors that are incompetent,” PAC chairman Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin said at a press conference in Parliament here. PAC said today’s report is still based on an early assessment, and the panel conceded that it has towait before it can say MAHB’s planned development roadmap is effective.

items.title: Johor polls bring unexpected boost for hawkers as campaign crowds drive sales
items.summary: KLUANG, July 1 — The Johor State Election Season is proving to be more than just a democratic exercise, as it has become an unexpected economic boost for petty traders and hawkers in the Layang-Layang and Simpang Renggam areas. For stall operator Noorma Zafmeeden, 70, from Felda Layang-Layang, the surge in customers during the campaign period has exceeded all expectations. She said her stall, which serves roti canai and nasi lemak, would normally generate less than RM400 in morning sales, but business has increased significantly since the start of the election campaign.

items.title: Wildlife attacks, pests caused over RM1.4m in farm losses in 2025-2026, deputy minister says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — Deputy Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Chan Foong Hin said wildlife attacks and pest infestations continue to pose a significant threat to Malaysia’s agricultural sector, with losses recorded across farms. He said the government introduced the Property and Crop Loss Assistance Initiative Due to Wildlife Attacks (BKHT), jointly implemented by the ministry and the Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability, to support farmers, fishers and aquaculture operators affected by wildlife damage. “Under BKHT, RM396,000 in assistance was approved in 2025 for 131 farmers, with estimated losses totalling RM808,000.

items.title: Johor polls: Candidates turn to hybrid campaigning as digital politics reshapes voter outreach
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 1 — Election campaigning has shifted dramatically from the traditional politik balai raya (community hall politics) to a hybrid model that blends grassroots engagement with digital outreach, as candidates seek to win over an increasingly diverse electorate. “Urban constituencies with high internet penetration are well-suited to digital campaigns that enable information to spread quickly, while rural areas with limited connectivity still require candidates to engage voters in person to build interpersonal relationships. Another political analyst, Dr Norman Sarpan, said political parties and candidates have little choice but to embrace digital platforms to remain relevant in today’s political landscape.

items.title: Anwar calls for fairer global financial system as AI reshapes finance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today called for a fairer global financial system as artificial intelligence reshapes finance, saying new technologies should help reduce economic inequalities instead of reinforcing existing imbalances. Speaking at the launch of Ant International’s Global Development Centre here, Anwar said the current global financial system had largely favoured multinational corporations while leaving many developing economies and small businesses vulnerable to external shocks. “Much needed are innovations that strengthen resilience, reduce exposure to international shocks, and fairly provide micro-credits, as we weather through financial storms and global economic crisis,” he said.

items.title: Italy eyes bigger JS-SEZ presence as European investor interest gathers pace, says Akmal Nasrullah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — Italy is preparing to bring more investors to explore opportunities in the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) as European interest in Malaysia’s flagship cross-border economic corridor continues to grow. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the government organised the JS-SEZ Executive Forum after receiving strong interest from Italy and other European countries seeking to better understand investment opportunities in the economic zone. Akmal said he had personally committed to organising the engagement after Italian Ambassador Raffaele Langella informed him that investors were keen to explore opportunities in the JS-SEZ.

items.title: Economy minister: JS-SEZ blueprint completed, awaits Anwar-Lawrence Wong joint launch
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — The Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) blueprint has been completed and approved by the Cabinet but will only be officially launched jointly by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Singapore Prime Minister Lawrence Wong. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the blueprint had already been finalised as an operational document, with implementation already underway despite the formal launch still pending. “That is why Cabinet decided that Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, together with Prime Minister Lawrence Wong, should launch the document,” he said.

items.title: Sarawak, Brunei police strengthen cooperation to tackle cross-border security challenges
items.summary: MIRI, July 1 — Sarawak Police Commissioner Datuk Mohamad Zainal Abdullah led a delegation from the Sarawak Police Contingent to a bilateral relations meeting with the Royal Brunei Police Force (PPDB) at its headquarters in Bandar Seri Begawan yesterday. The Sarawak delegation comprised senior officers, including Sarawak Deputy Police Commissioner DCP Saifullizan Ishak; Sarawak Police Contingent Head of the Management Department SAC Zulkipli Suhaili; Head of the Internal Security and Public Order Department SAC Ku Mashariman Ku Mahmood; Sarawak General Operations Force (GOF) Commander SAC Lim Bak Phai; Sarawak Marine Police Region 5 Commander ACP Ab Rahman Mat Hasan; Sarawak Air Operations Force Base Commander ACP Faizul Mohammad; as well as the district police chiefs of Miri, Limbang and Lawas. Meanwhile, the PPDB delegation was led by Brunei Police Commissioner Dato Seri Pahlawan Sulaiman Alidin, accompanied by his deputy Pengiran Anak Mohamad Saifullah Pengiran Indera Setia Diraja Sahibul Karib Pengiran Anak Idris, along with the force’s top leadership.

items.title: Johor JPJ introduces multi-counters for faster road tax and licence renewals
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 1 — The Johor Road Transport Department (JPJ) has set up four multi-counters at its headquarters in Taman Daya here to handle individual private Motor Vehicle Licence (LKM) and Malaysian Driving Licence (LMM) renewal transactions starting today. State RTD director Zulkarnain Yasin said the initiative allows customers who want to renew both documents to take only one queue number and get services at the same counter. “In addition, customers can also save time and thus further streamline LKM and LMM renewal transactions,” he said in a statement today.
